title: Tracking a Rip Current Dye Release
text:
Field Research · Long Beach, BC
Tracking a Rip Current Dye Release
Drone-derived shapefile analysis  ·  Surf zone hydrodynamics  ·  2025




About the Study
Fluorescent dye was released into an active rip current at Long Beach, British Columbia,
        and tracked via drone imagery at regular intervals. Each shapefile represents the extent
        of the dye plume at a discrete moment in time. Use the slider or press play to animate the sequence.





Lovekin Rock — Long Beach, BC



Shoreline — study site foreshore




Interactive Plume Map
Step through each timestep using the slider. The panel below shows plume area in m² and rate of change from the previous frame.
